name: Boldr — Workflow 1: Email Processing

parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 1500, system: $json.system_prompt, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Process this customer enquiry and return JSON only.

Customer name: ' + $json.customer_name + '
Customer email: ' + $json.customer_email + '
Subject: ' + $json.email_subject + '
Message:
' + $json.email_body }] }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ ticket_id: $json.ticket_id, received_at: $json.received_at, customer_name: $json.customer_name, customer_email: $json.customer_email, channel: 'email', subject: $json.email_subject, message_body: $json.email_body, question_type: $json.question_type, intent_summary: $json.intent_summary || null, answerable: false, persona_tag: $json.raw_persona_tag || null, competition_persona: $json.competition_persona_tag || null, theme: $json.theme || null, marketing_signal: false, draft_reply: null, kb_sources_used: [], gap_flag: false, order_escalated: true }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ ticket_id: $json.ticket_id, received_at: $json.received_at, customer_name: $json.customer_name, customer_email: $json.customer_email, channel: 'email', subject: $json.email_subject, message_body: $json.email_body, question_type: $json.question_type, intent_summary: $json.intent_summary || null, answerable: true, persona_tag: $json.raw_persona_tag || null, competition_persona: $json.competition_persona_tag || null, theme: $json.theme || null, marketing_signal: $json.marketing_signal || false, draft_reply: $json.draft_reply || null, kb_sources_used: $json.kb_sources_used || [], gap_flag: false, order_escalated: false }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ gap_id: 'GAP-' + Date.now(), ticket_id: $json.ticket_id, question_summary: $json.gap_summary || $json.intent_summary || 'No summary', theme: $json.theme || 'other', persona_tag: $json.raw_persona_tag || null, marketing_signal: $json.marketing_signal || false, kb_draft_status: 'pending' }) }}

={{ JSON.stringify({ ticket_id: $json.ticket_id, received_at: $json.received_at, customer_name: $json.customer_name, customer_email: $json.customer_email, channel: 'email', subject: $json.email_subject, message_body: $json.email_body, question_type: $json.question_type, intent_summary: $json.intent_summary || null, answerable: false, persona_tag: $json.raw_persona_tag || null, competition_persona: $json.competition_persona_tag || null, theme: $json.theme || null, marketing_signal: $json.marketing_signal || false, draft_reply: null, kb_sources_used: [], gap_flag: true, order_escalated: false }) }}

notes:
Option B — all credentials hardcoded (N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE workaround).
ROOT CAUSE: n8n task runner Code nodes cannot use $('NodeName') cross-references — only $input is available.
FIX: Two Set nodes bridge data across HTTP Request nodes (which replace their input with the response).
  • Set Email Fields: reads $('Gmail Trigger').first().json, appends customer_name/email/subject/body to each chunk item
  • Attach Email Fields: reads $('Build KB Context').first().json, appends email fields back after Claude API response replaces them
Code nodes now only use $input — no cross-node $() references.
Flow: Gmail Trigger → Fetch KB Chunks → Set Email Fields → Build KB Context → Claude API → Attach Email Fields → Parse Claude Response → Route by Type → [3 branches]
IMPORTANT: Replace REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_IMAP_CREDENTIAL_ID and REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_SLACK_CREDENTIAL_ID after import.
